hero.description: I'm passionate about designing backend systems, building secure REST APIs, and creating maintainable full-stack applications that solve real-world problems. From database design to deployment, I enjoy turning complex ideas into reliable software products.

projects.label: /04 FEATURED PROJECTS
projects.heading: Engineering Case Studies
projects.description: Each project represents real engineering challenges, from database design to production deployment.

entries.title: ANTPAY
entries.summary: Full-stack payment management internal system with invoice generation, transaction tracking, RBAC, and bilingual UI (Khmer/English).
details: Led a team of 5 to design and develop a full-stack payment management system featuring invoice generation, transaction tracking, role-based access control (RBAC), and a bilingual (Khmer/English) UI.

Developed 25+ RESTful APIs using Node.js, Express, and MySQL, implementing JWT authentication, RBAC, invoice management, overdue tracking, expense and budget management, audit logging, centralized error handling, request validation, and reusable API response utilities.
Designed a normalized MySQL database schema and deployed the application on Linux using PM2 and Nginx, collaborating through Git and GitHub.

entries.title: VETIKA
entries.summary: ANT student community web platform featuring authentication, profile management, news feeds, and real-time messaging.
details: Led a team of five to develop VETIKA, a student community platform with authentication, profile management, news feeds, and real-time messaging.
Developed the administration module with a Content Management System (CMS), analytics dashboard, role-based management, and CSV/PDF report generation.

entries.title: Smakjit (Volunteer)
entries.summary: Volunteer community platform built from static HTML into a production-ready full-stack application for the RUPP Year 3 capstone project.
details: Built Smakjit from a static HTML/CSS/Bootstrap site into a production-ready full-stack volunteer platform for the RUPP Year 3 capstone project.
Developed the frontend using Next.js and the backend using PHP/Laravel, implementing volunteer registration, authentication, and project management features.

entries.title: Smart Document Q&A and Workflow Assistant
entries.summary: AI-powered smart document assistant integrating text extraction, semantic search, vector databases, and interactive conversational Q&A.
details: Built an AI-powered assistant enabling natural language queries over document repositories using TF-IDF and embedding-based semantic retrieval.
Integrated Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to generate context-aware answers, summaries, and verifiable references from uploaded PDFs and Word files.
Developed a structured data exporter converting unstructured documents into JSON, CSV, searchable OCR PDFs, and multi-sheet Excel files.

entries.title: SEO Preview
entries.summary: A real-time search engine optimization (SEO) metadata previewer to visualize website search results on Google, Facebook, and Twitter.
details: Built a real-time SEO metadata preview studio to visualize desktop/mobile SERP search results for Google and Bing.
Implemented an interactive editor panel with live character counters, Open Graph preview cards (Facebook, LinkedIn), canonical URLs, and robots directives.
Created live scoring systems, checklist audits, automated HTML meta-tag generators, and clipboard export utilities using pure vanilla ES6 JavaScript.

entries.title: Assignment Web
entries.summary: A role-based online assignment management system built with Flask, SQLAlchemy, and Bootstrap, supporting Admin, Teacher, and Student roles.
details: Developed a role-based online assignment management system with Flask blueprints, SQLAlchemy ORM, and Flask-Login authentication.
Supported three distinct user personas: Admin (managing Whitelist and Users), Teacher (class and grading setups), and Student (enrolling and submitting uploads).
Implemented CSRF token validation, input sanitization, file size verification (up to 16MB), and custom error handlers.

principles.label: /05 ENGINEERING PRINCIPLES
principles.heading: How I Think About Software
principles.description: These principles guide every architectural decision, every API endpoint, and every line of code I write.

entries.title: API FIRST
entries.description: Design reliable APIs that serve as the foundation of scalable software systems.

entries.title: SECURITY BY DESIGN
entries.description: Authentication, authorization, validation, and secure coding practices are part of development — not afterthoughts.

entries.title: CLEAN ARCHITECTURE
entries.description: Readable, maintainable, and scalable code always outlives shortcuts.

entries.title: SYSTEM THINKING
entries.description: Understand how every component works together, from database schema to deployment pipeline.

entries.title: CONTINUOUS LEARNING
entries.description: Technology evolves quickly. Learning never stops. Every project is an opportunity to improve.

architecture.label: /06 ARCHITECTURE
architecture.heading: Engineering Reliable Software Systems
architecture.description: Every feature begins with architecture. Every architecture decision affects maintainability, performance, security, and long-term scalability.
architecture.quote: Turning complex systems into simple experiences.
architecture.apiText: REST APIs are designed with consistency, predictability, and maintainability in mind. Every endpoint follows clear naming conventions, proper HTTP methods, structured validation, meaningful status codes, and consistent response formats.
architecture.dbText: Database design begins before writing code. Tables model real-world relationships while remaining normalized, maintainable, and scalable.
architecture.ariaDesc: The system architecture follows an 8-layer pipeline design: User/Client sends requests to the REST API layer built with Express.js. Requests pass through Authentication middleware using JWT, then Authorization using RBAC. Controllers handle validation, Services contain business logic, Repositories abstract data access, and MySQL provides persistent storage.

nodes.label: LAYER 2
nodes.title: REST API
nodes.description: Central routing layer that dispatches incoming requests.

nodes.label: LAYER 3
nodes.title: Authentication
nodes.description: Verifies identity before protected resources can be accessed.

nodes.label: LAYER 4
nodes.title: Authorization
nodes.description: Determines what authenticated users are allowed to do.

nodes.label: LAYER 5
nodes.title: Controllers
nodes.description: Handles request validation and orchestrates service calls.

nodes.label: LAYER 6
nodes.title: Services
nodes.description: Contains core business logic and business rules.

nodes.label: LAYER 7
nodes.title: Repository
nodes.description: Abstracts database interactions behind a clean interface.

nodes.label: LAYER 8
nodes.title: Database
nodes.description: Persistent storage layer for all application data.
